title: incompatible_upgrade
description: A software upgrade introduces incompatibilities that break dependent services.

timeline.title: Upgrade Deployed
timeline.description: A database engine upgrade is applied during a maintenance window.

timeline.title: Application Errors Begin
timeline.description: Multiple applications start reporting connection errors and deprecated API failures immediately after the upgrade.

timeline.title: Scope Assessment
timeline.description: Teams identify that 8 applications are affected by breaking changes in the new database API.

timeline.title: Rollback vs. Fix Decision
timeline.description: Engineers assess whether to roll back the database or to rapidly patch the affected applications.

timeline.title: Application Patches Applied
timeline.description: Application teams apply compatibility patches and test against the new database version.

timeline.title: All Services Restored
timeline.description: All 8 applications are updated and operating correctly with the new database version.

node-1.title: Post-Upgrade Application Failures
node-1.situation: After a database engine upgrade from version 5.7 to 8.0, 8 applications immediately start reporting errors including deprecated SQL functions and changed authentication protocol requirements. The upgrade was not preceded by adequate compatibility testing.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Halt all application deployments, notify application teams and stakeholders, and quickly assess the severity and scope of the incompatibilities to decide between rollback or forward-fix.

options.label: B
options.text: Immediately roll back the database engine without assessing the impact of rollback on data written since the upgrade.

options.label: C
options.text: Ignore the errors and wait to see if applications adapt on their own after a few minutes.

node-2.title: Rollback vs. Forward Fix
node-2.situation: Assessment shows: the database rollback would require reverting 45 minutes of transaction data; the 8 affected applications each need relatively minor code changes (deprecated function replacements); 3 of the 8 apps have active on-call engineers available. A rollback is risky due to data loss.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Choose the forward-fix path: coordinate all 8 application teams to apply compatibility patches, prioritize the 3 with available engineers first, and use a compatibility layer for applications whose teams are not immediately available.

options.label: B
options.text: Roll back the database despite the 45-minute data loss, affecting production data integrity.

node-3.title: Application Patching Coordination
node-3.situation: Three applications have been patched and are running correctly. Five applications are still failing. For these five, you can use a MySQL 5.7 compatibility mode flag available in MySQL 8.0 as a temporary bridge while their teams prepare patches.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Enable MySQL 5.7 compatibility mode for the five remaining applications as a temporary measure, restore their functionality, and coordinate their teams to apply proper patches within 48 hours.

options.label: B
options.text: Leave the five remaining applications failing until their teams can apply patches, even if it takes several days.

node-4.title: Full Resolution and Process Review
node-4.situation: All 8 applications are now operational. The three using compatibility mode must be properly patched within 48 hours. Management asks why compatibility testing did not catch these issues before production deployment.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Document the incident, implement a requirement for compatibility matrix testing before major upgrades, establish a staging environment that mirrors production dependencies, and track the remaining 5 app patches to completion.

options.label: B
options.text: Close the incident after all applications are functional and rely on the same upgrade process for future upgrades.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You managed the incompatible upgrade by choosing a forward-fix approach to avoid data loss, using compatibility modes as a temporary bridge for slower teams, and establishing improved upgrade testing procedures to prevent future incidents.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
